instructions.instruction: Check REVIEWED.md and references/REFERENCE_INDEX.md for duplicates before creating any file
instructions.original_snippets: Search REVIEWED.md and references/REFERENCE_INDEX.md for the slug or repo URL. If already present, report it and stop.
instructions.relevant_when: User provides a GitHub URL, npm/PyPI package, or tool name to triage

instructions.instruction: Derive a stable slug using <author-or-org>-<tool-name> convention; use just the tool name for well-known single-name tools
instructions.original_snippets: Derive a stable slug: <author-or-org>-<tool-name>. For well-known single-name tools, use just the tool name (e.g. context-mode, memv).
instructions.relevant_when: Before creating any file for a new tool

instructions.instruction: Confirm scope relevance before investing in a full write-up; a well-reasoned out-of-scope ruling is as valuable as a full summary
instructions.original_snippets: Scope before summary: confirm the tool is relevant to the research domain before investing in a full write-up.
instructions.relevant_when: After resolving the source and before filling in the reference summary

instructions.instruction: Quote README and docs only; never infer capabilities not described by the author
instructions.original_snippets: Evidence first: quote README and docs; never infer capabilities not described by the author.
instructions.relevant_when: Filling in all sections of references/<slug>.md

instructions.instruction: Create references/<slug>.md using templates/REFERENCE-tool.md and populate every section
instructions.original_snippets: Create references/<slug>.md using templates/REFERENCE-tool.md. Populate every section.
instructions.relevant_when: After duplicate check passes and scope is confirmed

instructions.original_snippets: TL;DR: 3–8 bullets capturing what the tool does and why it matters.

instructions.instruction: Always mark self-reported benchmarks as (as reported) — never state them as verified facts
instructions.original_snippets: Self-reported metrics: quote numbers with source — always mark (as reported).
instructions.relevant_when: Filling in the Self-reported metrics section

instructions.instruction: Never run git submodule add during triage — offer vendoring as a follow-up after the summary is written
instructions.original_snippets: Triage-then-promote: vendoring both require explicit user confirmation — never automatic.
instructions.relevant_when: When the tool warrants code-level inspection

instructions.instruction: Add a reverse-chronological row to REVIEWED.md summary table
instructions.original_snippets: Add a row to the summary table at the top (reverse-chronological).

instructions.instruction: Never create ANALYSIS-*.md automatically — offer promotion as an option and wait for user confirmation
instructions.original_snippets: Promotion to ANALYSIS-*.md is a quality gate, not automatic.
instructions.relevant_when: At the end of every triage workflow
